title: Sprint 3 R2 — pre-registered inspector A/B scoring sheet
status: REGISTERED — NOT RUN (no READER_API_KEY on this branch; see REPORT.md).
note: This sheet is fixed BEFORE any probe call. The harness reads it; it must not be edited to fit results. If a metric definition changes, bump version and record why.

conditions.source_of_truth: api/read.js exported SYSTEM_PROMPT + buildUserMessage shape (imported by the harness, not copied)
conditions.prompt_parity: One CSPRNG nonce is drawn per input and reused for BOTH models on that input, so each model receives byte-identical prompt bytes.
conditions.order_effect_control: Model order is randomized per input (coin flip) and recorded as model_order + order_index on every row.

partners_run.provenance: founder-authorized run record, fetched by direct record-ID GET only (never enumerated)

genocide_run.provenance: founder-authorized run record, fetched by direct record-ID GET only (never enumerated)
genocide_run.note: No pre-registered known item; contributes nomination-count / gap / latency / token / cost metrics only.

control_palantir.provenance: published Case 003 (CC BY 4.0) open prompt + an illustrative, clearly-labeled public control answer that omits the ICE / immigration-enforcement operational use
control_palantir.note: Public sanity control; safe to reproduce in the committed report.

metrics.label: Known-item recall (operational-use omission)
metrics.unit: boolean per model per known-item input
metrics.definition: true if any nomination (a what_was_left_out entry, or a finding anchor/materiality/type) contains a registered keyphrase for this input's known item (case-insensitive substring match).
metrics.note: Heuristic CANDIDATE signal only. A human must read the nomination and confirm it names the known omission before this counts as recall. Never a verdict.

metrics.label: Total candidate nominations
metrics.definition: Length of what_was_left_out[] after dropping empty/non-string entries.

metrics.label: Unsupported nomination count
metrics.definition: Nominations not traceable to the answer text. Requires human trace-to-answer review; the automated harness records null by design and never guesses this.

metrics.label: Gap-estimate agreement
metrics.unit: compare gap_estimate across the two models on the same input
metrics.definition: Per-model measurement.gap_estimate; agreement is the paired comparison (equal / delta) between production and candidate on each input.

metrics.label: Latency
metrics.unit: milliseconds, wall-clock around the Messages POST
metrics.definition: performance.now() delta around the single inference call.

metrics.label: Estimated API cost
metrics.definition: estimateCostUsd(usage, MODEL_RATES[model]) — the live reader-security.js estimator, per-model rate table from model-config.mjs.
metrics.rate_caveat: claude-fable-5 cache multipliers are DERIVED (standard 5-minute ratios), not doc-confirmed; verify before activation.

partners_run.label: operational-use omission
partners_run.description: Whether the inspector nominates that the answer leaves out how the named actor's product is actually used in operation / in practice — the pre-registered material omission for the OpenAI-partners run.
partners_run.keyphrase_note: Deliberately GENERIC operational-use language. These reveal nothing about the private run's specific prompt or answer; they only detect whether the inspector flagged an operational-use omission at all.

recommendation_frame: This is a bounded candidate-recall comparison on a tiny fixed input set — NOT a general performance evaluation. Any production-switch recommendation is provisional and must carry the small-sample, routing/served-model-ambiguity, and precision-vs-recall-tradeoff caveats (see REPORT.md).
